ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #29, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis McQuaid and William Hoehler for Theodore Kolb.

Counsel explained that the property is a venue for weddings, issues with the neighbors, services provided by the Trustee, legal services provided, the benefits family members receive, how fees were calculated, that the property has appreciated in value, etc. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #29, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Continued To Dec-16-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #29, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Continued To Dec-02-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis McQuaid and William Hoehler for Co-Trustees. Tim Kneis (Paralegal) also present.

Mr. McQuaid explained that he had a discussion with the Probate Examiner about seven, eight or nine years with regard to there being so much detail in the accounting with rents, events, and employees, and that's when they decided to do the LLC to deal with all of the details. Mr. McQuaid also explained the amount of legal work required with managing the Estate, stated that he filed a declaration in support of fees, spoke of the size of the Estate (in acres), the events that are held at the Estate, stated that there are a number of facilities on the property which have leases, etc. Mr. Hoehler explained that the Trustees had an appraisal of the fee interests, and that there is no difference between the fair market value of the leased fee interests and the amounts in the accounting. Mr. Kneis stated that he prepared the accounting, explained that carry values with purchases and sales will change, and stated that the acquisitions are listed on a separate schedule. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #26, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-09-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis McQuaid for and with Theodore Kolb.

Mr. McQuaid stated that Mr. Kolb does not take any Trustees' fees, that he tries very hard to make sure time entries reflect legal fees, that he is managing the LLC, that the Trustees' meetings deal with the assets of the LLC and that Beneficiaries meet with the Trustees at the follow up meetings. In addtion, Mr. McQuaid explained that the line of credit is the only liability of the Estate, that the line of credit is used at the end of the year when they are waiting for a large payment from the vineyard and that it used for cash flow purposes. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #25
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: J. Dennis McQuaid for and with Theodore Kolb (Trustee).

The Court raised the issue of Mr. Kolb's legal fees, stated that some of the charges for legal fees could be the work of the Fiduciary and stated that they had a number of people, (accountants, property management co.) also doing the work of the Fiduciary. Mr. Kolb and Mr. McQuaid addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes, stated that the trustees fees were 1/2% of the old appraised value of the trust and explained the extent of services provided to support the Trustees' fees and the Attorney's fees being requested. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.
